title: ribes divaricatum var. inerme
intro: Ribes divaricatum var. inerme, also known as the naked thimbleberry, is a plant species belonging to the family Grossulariaceae, characterized by its thimble-shaped fruit and hairy, glandular stems. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ribes divaricatum var. inerme include its potential to alleviate urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
